text.title: Python Type Hints — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-type-hints application must obey.

rule.statement: Use built-in generics (list[int], dict[str, int]). Drop typing.List, Dict, Tuple imports.
rule.rationale: Built-in generics are the 3.12+ idiom; typing.* aliases are deprecated.

rule.statement: X | None instead of Optional[X]; X | Y instead of Union[X, Y].
rule.rationale: PEP 604 syntax is canonical 3.12+; no typing import needed.

rule.statement: Declare generics with PEP 695 syntax (def f[T](x: T) -> T) on 3.12+.
rule.rationale: PEP 695 attaches type parameters to the def, eliminating module-level TypeVar.

rule.statement: Run mypy/pyright in strict mode at module/package boundary. Allow per-module relaxation for legacy code only.
rule.rationale: Strict catches Optional misuse, untyped defs, implicit Any — cheapest defect detection layer.

rule.statement: Validate IO boundary (HTTP body, queue messages, DB rows) with Pydantic v2 BaseModel.
rule.rationale: Pydantic parses + validates + types in one step; the boundary is the highest-leverage place for validation.

rule.statement: Use object or # type: ignore[assignment] for truly unknown shapes. Reserve Any as a last resort with a justifying comment.
rule.rationale: Any disables type checking on every chained operation; object/unknown forces explicit narrowing.
